metadata.title: Build a Battery-Life Predictor with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. The model was accurate, the hardware worked, the firmware shipped — and the product died in the field because nobody multiplied two numbers before launch.

props.command: claude "write battery_life.py: inputs inference_ms, freq_hz, active_mA, sleep_uA, battery_mAh. Compute duty cycle, average current, days of life. Sweep frequency."
props.segment: Build a Battery-Life Predictor with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: The book's battery-life curve. Our CLI script predicts where your system lands on this curve — and whether inference frequency or radio transmission is the binding constraint.

beats.act: MANIM — Current strip: pulse period shrinks, days drop
beats.narration_text: Draw the current-versus-time strip. Each inference is a 22-millisecond active pulse, then back to sleep. At 0.033 Hz — once every 30 seconds — the duty cycle is 0.07 percent. Average current: 0.01 milliamps. A 220-milliamp-hour battery lasts 916 days. But as the period shrinks — frequency rises — the days counter drops fast.

The formula. Duty cycle equals inference milliseconds times frequency in hertz divided by 1000. Average current equals duty cycle times active milliamps plus one minus duty cycle times sleep microamps divided by 1000. Battery life in days equals capacity in milliamp-hours divided by average current times 24.

beats.act: MANIM — Frequency sweep: crossover at 7 days
beats.narration_text: Sweep frequency from 1 Hz to 100 Hz. At 10 Hz, duty cycle is 22 percent, average current is 3.3 milliamps, battery life collapses to 2.8 days. The crossover where life drops below 7 days — that is your design constraint. Run the predictor before writing firmware.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write battery_life.py — inputs: inference milliseconds, frequency in hertz, active milliamps, sleep microamps, and battery capacity in milliamp-hours. Compute duty cycle, average current, and days of life. Sweep frequency. Run it before writing firmware. Then add a sweep from 1 Hz to 100 Hz and find the frequency where battery life crosses 7 days — that is your design constraint.

props.command: claude "write battery_life.py: inputs inference_ms, freq_hz, active_mA, sleep_uA, battery_mAh. Compute duty cycle, average current, days of life. Sweep frequency."

props.title: Build a Battery-Life Predictor with Claude Code
